sourceMovie.title: Witness
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Witness for the same reason it broke through in 1985: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Peter Weir keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Prisoners
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Witness clicked for you, Prisoners (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Witness?
faq.answer: Try Prisoners, The Talented Mr. Ripley, A History of Violence—each shares DNA with Witness while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Witness?
faq.answer: Witness (1985) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Witness worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
